The primary rationale for this alignment lies not only in the rapid recruitment of neutrophils but also because they initiate an early phase of inflammation necessary for addressing potential infections before more specialized cells arrive. Additionally, studies have shown that even minor injuries can trigger robust neutrophil responses quickly, underscoring their pivotal role during these first moments after damage occurs.

Answer choice: A.
